member.name: Mr Andrew Snowden
member.synopsis: Mr Andrew Snowden is the Conservative MP for Fylde , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024. He currently undertakes the role of Opposition Assistant Whip (Commons).

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Youth Services: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, how the £500 million allocated to the National Youth Strategy will be profiled over the spending period; and what steps she is taking to ensure the funding is spent equitably across regions.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Youth Matters will be focussed on ensuring young people have someone who cares, something to do and somewhere to go. The majority of our funding will be targeted to underserved areas who need it most. We are committed to working with areas of greatest nee...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Extracurricular Activities: Public Participation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of the National Youth Strategy on levels of youth participation in out-of-school activities.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We’re working closely across government to expand access to meaningful and enriching activities as we know that strong offline connections between young people are essential to supporting their wellbeing and their development. In December 2025, DCMS publi...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Confectionery
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment his Department has made of the potential impact of the proposed 2018 Nutrient Profiling Model on manufacturers of confectionery and sweet-packaged foods.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The new Nutrient Profiling Model (NPM) is built on the latest science and has been updated in line with the latest dietary advice, particularly in relation to free sugars, which are more strongly correlated with poor health outcomes, including obesity.The...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: UK Town of Culture
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what estimate she has made of the total cost of the UK Town of Culture 2028 competition, including (a) prize funding, (b) development grants, (c) administration and (d) assessment costs.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In launching the UK Town of Culture competition, the government announced that each of the shortlisted towns will receive £60,000 to help deliver full bids for competition, the winner will receive a £3 million prize; and two runners up will receive £250,0...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: UK Town of Culture
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, who has been appointed to the Expert Advisory Panel for the UK Town of Culture 2028 competition.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Expert Advisory Panel provides critical and objective assessment of bids against published criteria set by DCMS, and makes recommendations to the Secretary of State for Culture, Media, and Sport at each competition stage. The Panel is made up of a ran...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: UK Town of Culture
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, how many Expressions of Interest for the UK Town of Culture were deemed ineligible, and for what reasons.
writtenQuestions.answerText: None of the Expressions of Interest were deemed ineligible.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: UK Town of Culture
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what assessment she has made of the geographic distribution of Expressions of Interest received.
writtenQuestions.answerText: DCMS received 398 Expression of Interests representing over 400 places from across the UK. I am delighted that so many towns wanted to celebrate their contribution to our national story and to use culture and creativity to tell their story and why they ar...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Teachers: Recruitment
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, how many additional teachers the Government expects to be recruited by the end of the Parliament.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This government is committed to recruiting 6,500 new expert teachers as part of our Plan for Change, because we know that high-quality teaching is the biggest in-school and college factor that contributes to better pupil outcomes.In February, we published...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Headteachers: Labour Turnover
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, which areas will participate in the place-based Headteacher Retention Incentive pilot.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Headteachers and leaders are central to driving improvement in schools and ensuring all children achieve and thrive no matter where they are in the country. To attract and support the best headteachers to stay in the areas that need them most, the departm...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Sustainable Farming Incentive: Fylde
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, how many farms in Fylde constituency he expects to benefit from the reopening of the Sustainable Farming Incentive.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As of 06 July, there are 68 farm businesses in Fylde constituency that already have a Sustainable Farming Incentive agreement. Based on the current eligibility criteria, there are 123 farm businesses in the Fylde constituency that are expected to be eligi...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Agriculture: Investment
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, who will be appointed to the task-and-finish group on private finance in sustainable farming.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Membership of the Taskforce will be by invitation, and the Government will ensure representation of key sectors and expertise. Membership will not constitute a formal public appointment and will not be paid. Further details will be available in due course...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Food: Production
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of the farming road map on domestic food production.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Roadmap recognises maintaining domestic food production at least at current levels is integral to our national security. It supports domestic food production by giving farmers long-term clarity to plan, invest and adapt. It sets out Government’s role ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Agriculture: Innovation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what steps her Department is taking to ensure that additional funding for the Farming Innovation Programme is allocated equitably by region.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Since 2021, the Farming Innovation Programme has been Defra’s flagship for supporting research and innovation in farming and our investment in the research and development pipeline of innovative technology from concept to deployment aims to derisk adoptio...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Asylum: Sponsorship
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what safeguards will be put in place to help ensure that organisations sponsoring refugees under the community sponsorship scheme are resourced to provide housing, integration and employment support.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As announced on 27 June, the refugee study and named sponsorship routes will open to applications from prospective lead sponsors in the autumn, with arrivals expected from autumn 2027 onwards. The refugee work route is expected to open next year.The Home ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Asylum: Sponsorship
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what criteria will be used to accredit organisations acting as lead sponsors under the proposed community sponsorship scheme for refugees.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As announced on 27 June, the refugee study and named sponsorship routes will open to applications from prospective lead sponsors in the autumn, with arrivals expected from autumn 2027 onwards. The refugee work route is expected to open next year.The Home ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Asylum
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what the timetable is for the rollout of new safe and legal routes for refugees.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As announced on 27 June, the refugee study and named sponsorship routes will open to applications from prospective lead sponsors in the autumn, with arrivals expected from autumn 2027 onwards. The refugee work route is expected to open next year.The Home ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Civil Service: Greater Manchester
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, what steps he is taking to ensure civil servants in the wider north west benefit from the construction of the Manchester Digital Campus.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Manchester Digital Campus (MDC) will bring together approximately 8,800 civil servants from multiple government departments, with a focus on digital delivery. A significant proportion will create new roles in the region.As a centre of digital expertise, M...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Cabinet Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Higher Education: English Language
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, with reference to the written statement of 25 June 2026 on Student outcomes in higher education, HCWS150, what level of English language proficiency is being considered as part of the proposed consultation.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department’s consultation will seek views on whether and if so, how, a clear, consistent baseline of English language proficiency could be used as a condition of access to student finance to strengthen protections across the system. Our aim is to prot...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Puberty Suppressing Hormones: Clinical Trials
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether his Department received representations from external organisations regarding the MHRA's review of the PATHWAYS clinical trial protocol.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department regularly receives correspondence from external organisations relating to the PATHWAYS clinical trial, some of which addresses the clinical trial protocol. However, the review of the PATHWAYS clinical trial protocol was conducted independen...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Erasmus+ Programme
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what estimate her Department has made of the cost per placement on the Erasmus+ in each of the first five years of association.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK will contribute around £570 million to the Erasmus+ programme in 2027. This is down from the approximately £810 million we would have paid under default terms.This commitment covers the 2027/28 academic year. Any participation in Erasmus+ into the ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education
